document.docAuthor: Peng, Feng, Xie, Xiufeng, Peng, Zhengqiang, Wang, Xingmin & Chen, Xiaoshen
document.masterDocTitle: A taxonomic review of the genus Axinoscymnus Kamiya, with descriptions of three new species (Coleoptera, Coccinellidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A taxonomic review of the genus Axinoscymnus Kamiya, with descriptions of three new species (Coleoptera, Coccinellidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Key Laboratory of Bio-Pesticide Innovation and Application, Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510642, China. & Engineering Research Center of Biocontrol, Ministry of Education and Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510640, China.

Key Laboratory of Bio-Pesticide Innovation and Application, Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510642, China. & Engineering Research Center of Biocontrol, Ministry of Education and Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510640, China.

Key Laboratory of Bio-Pesticide Innovation and Application, Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510642, China. & Engineering Research Center of Biocontrol, Ministry of Education and Guangdong Province, Guangzhou 510640, China.

bibRefCitation.author: Kamiya, H.
bibRefCitation.refString: Kamiya, H. (1965) Coccinellid-Fauna of the Ryukyu Islands, south of the Amami Group (Coleoptera). Kontyu, 33 (1), 97 - 122.

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Axinoscymnus nigripennis Kamiya, 1965: 100
.—
Sasaji 1971: 115
,
Yang et Wu 1972: 125
,
Ren et Pang 1992: 21
,
Pang et al . 2004: 90
.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Diagnosis. This species is similar to
A. pingxiangicus Peng et Chen
,
sp. n.
in external appearance and male genitalia but can be separated from the latter by the ventrite 1 with coarse punctures at middle ( Fig. 12d ), and the penis guide swollen at base in lateral view ( Fig. 12f ). It also resembles
A. rai Kamiya
in the shape of male genitalia but can be easily distinguished from the latter by the black elytra ( Fig. 12a ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 12.
Axinoscymnus nigripennis Kamiya, 1965
: a dorsal view b lateral view c frontal view d abdomen e penis f tegmen, lateral view g tegmen, inner view. Scale bars: a–c: 0.5 mm, d: 0.1 mm, e–g: 0.05 mm.

Distribution. China ( Guangxi , Guizhou , Hunan , Anhui , Hainan , Guangdong , Taiwan , Hognkong); Laos ; Japan .
Remarks. This species is recorded from Laos for the first time.
